prompt:
Implement bidirectional Google Chat integration for reel-life, replacing the current incoming-webhook-only approach with a proper Google Chat App that has its own identity.

## What exists today
- POST /webhook endpoint in cmd/reel-life/main.go is scaffolded but returns 200 OK without processing
- Proactive monitoring sends alerts via incoming webhook URL (chat.NewGoogleChat in internal/chat/google.go)
- Agent.Process(ctx, message) in internal/agent/agent.go is ready to handle user messages
- Notifier interface in internal/chat/chat.go has Send() and SendThread()

## What to implement

### 1. Google Chat event handling (cmd/reel-life/main.go)
- Parse Google Chat HTTP event payloads on POST /webhook
- Handle event types: MESSAGE (user sends text), ADDED_TO_SPACE, REMOVED_FROM_SPACE
- Extract the user's message text from MESSAGE events
- Route message text to agent.Process()
- Return the agent's response as a synchronous JSON reply (Google Chat expects a Message JSON response from the webhook within 30s)
- For ADDED_TO_SPACE, return a welcome message
- Verify the request is from Google Chat using the Bearer token in the Authorization header (validate JWT against Google's public keys for chat@system.gserviceaccount.com)

### 2. Google Chat API client for async responses (internal/chat/)
- Add a new GoogleChatApp implementation of the Notifier interface that uses the Google Chat REST API (spaces.messages.create) with service account auth instead of incoming webhook URLs
- Use Google service account JSON key file for authentication (google.golang.org/api/chat/v1 or raw REST calls with oauth2)
- Support sending messages to a space, replying in threads
- The existing GoogleChat webhook notifier should remain as a fallback/option, but the new app-based notifier becomes the primary path when service account credentials are configured

### 3. Configuration updates (internal/config/)
- Add config fields for the Chat App mode:
  - chat.service_account_file (path to JSON key, or via GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_FILE env var)
  - chat.space (the Google Chat space resource name, e.g. spaces/XXXXXX)
  - chat.project_number (for JWT audience validation)
- Keep backward compatibility: if only webhook_url is set, use the existing webhook notifier
- If service_account_file + space are set, use the new Chat API notifier

### 4. Tests
- Integration-style tests for the webhook handler using httptest — POST realistic Google Chat event payloads and verify agent is called and response is correct JSON
- Test JWT validation with a mock token (or skip validation in test mode)
- Test the Chat API notifier with httptest mock server
- Test config loading with new fields
- Run the full test suite (go test ./...) and ensure everything passes before creating the PR

## Key design decisions
- Use synchronous webhook responses for the primary request/response flow (Google Chat posts event, reel-life returns Message JSON). This avoids needing async message creation for the basic flow.
- Use the Chat API client (async) for proactive monitor alerts and any follow-up messages
- Keep the Notifier interface — the new Chat API client implements it
- Prefer google.golang.org/api/chat/v1 if it simplifies auth, otherwise raw REST + golang.org/x/oauth2/google is fine
- Minimize dependencies — use the standard Google API client libraries

## Architecture reference
- CLAUDE.md has build/test/lint commands and architecture overview
- internal/chat/chat.go defines the Notifier interface
- internal/chat/google.go is the current webhook implementation
- internal/agent/agent.go has Agent.Process() 
- cmd/reel-life/main.go wires everything together
